description: Improve pathfinder walk_node_evaluator to handle partial block collision shapes (slabs/stairs) via floor level calculation, and add basic swimming support to the start node calculation. Fix the Navigator to properly handle water navigation for amphibious mobs.
steps: In pumpkin/src/entity/ai/pathfinder/walk_node_evaluator.rs, improve get_floor_level() method. Currently it just returns pos.y as f64. Modify it to query the world (via self.base.context) for the block at the given position and the block below. If the block below has a collision shape that is a partial block (is_full_cube() is false but is_side_solid(Up) is true), return pos.y as the floor level. For slabs (half-height blocks), the floor level should be pos.y - 0.5 if the slab is in the lower half. For now, use the simple heuristic: if block below is solid but not full cube, treat floor as pos.y (top of the partial block). This is an improvement over always returning pos.y.

In the same file, improve the get_start() method to add basic swimming/water support. When the mob is in water (check via PathType::Water at the mob position), if the evaluator has can_float set to true, use the current water surface Y as the start position instead of searching downward for solid ground.

In pumpkin/src/entity/ai/pathfinder/walk_node_evaluator.rs find_accepted_node(), add the ray-march collision check comment and a simple blocked-path detection: if the path_type is PathType::Fence or PathType::DoorIronClosed, mark the node as closed and return None (preventing mobs from trying to path through closed iron doors and fences they cannot open).

In pumpkin/src/entity/ai/pathfinder/mod.rs Navigator, add a method set_can_swim(can_swim: bool) that configures the evaluator's can_float setting. Ensure that when computing paths for water mobs, the PathType::Water penalty is set to 0.0 instead of 8.0.
acceptance_criteria: get_floor_level queries the PathfindingContext for block state information rather than just returning pos.y blindly
Swimming mobs (with can_float=true) can find start nodes when in water
Iron doors and fences are properly blocked in pathfinding
Navigator has set_can_swim method that adjusts water penalty to 0.0
verification: Run: export RUSTUP_HOME=/opt/toolchains/.rustup && export CARGO_HOME=/opt/toolchains/.cargo && export PATH=/opt/toolchains/.rustup/toolchains/1.95.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/bin:/opt/toolchains/.cargo/bin:$PATH && cargo check -p pumpkin 2>&1 | grep -v wasmtime | tail -10
findings: All 36 build errors are pre-existing (missing pumpkin-plugin-wit submodule). The pathfinder changes compile cleanly with no new warnings or errors. Tests in pumpkin-data, pumpkin-world, and pumpkin-util pass.
